title: onosmodium molle subsp. hispidissimum
intro: Onosmodium molle subsp. hispidissimum, also known as the hispid False Penstemon, is a plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae and is a perennial herb with small white or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Onosmodium molle subsp. hispidissimum can be used to treat various health issues, including pain relief, fever reduction, and respiratory problems, and may also possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
